items.title: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is read server-side via config; the engine fails fast without it
items.precondition: Server .env has ANTHROPIC_API_KEY set; key never reaches the Vite bundle.
steps: 1. Confirm requireAnthropicApiKey() in src/config/index.ts reads config.anthropicApiKey and throws a clear error when empty.
2. Confirm the agent (src/agents/interviewAgent.ts) constructs the SDK only via requireAnthropicApiKey() (server-side).
3. Grep frontend/src for the key — it must not appear; only VITE_ vars ship (check:conventions §17.3 advisory).
items.expected: Key is server-side only; a missing key fails fast with a readable message; no key in the frontend bundle.
items.evidence: Live run constructed claude-opus-4-8 with the configured key. check:conventions --strict reported 0 violations incl. §17.3 (no process.env / secrets in frontend). Key referenced only in src/config + src/agents.

items.title: Batch generation returns <=4 dependency-ordered questions as validated structured output
items.precondition: A session exists (POST /sessions). Agent SDK mocked for the suite; real model for the live run.
steps: 1. POST /sessions/:id/interview/next-batch.
2. Inspect data.turns[0].questions.questions — each has id, decisionKey, text, options[], allowOther, dependsOn.
3. Submit an over-long (5-question) batch in a unit test and confirm it is rejected (BATCH_GENERATION_INVALID), not persisted.
items.expected: A well-formed batch of at most 4 questions is returned and persisted; malformed/over-long model output is rejected at the boundary and never written.
items.evidence: Live run generated 4 questions (data_scope, export_trigger, access_permissions, format_details). Service tests: 'rejects an over-long batch (>4)' and 'rejects malformed (off-schema) model output' both pass — turns table stays empty.

items.title: Write-through persistence is atomic; resume replays saved state with no regeneration
items.precondition: A session with one generated batch.
steps: 1. POST /sessions/:id/interview/answers with answers for the open batch.
2. Read interview_turns + decision_record for the session — the turn carries answers and one decision_record row per answered question (source=answer).
3. GET /sessions/:id/interview again (simulating resume) and confirm it rebuilds from the DB with no new model call.
items.expected: Turn answers and decision rows are written in one transaction; resume returns the exact persisted turns + decisions; no batch is regenerated.
items.evidence: Live run persisted turns=1, decisions=4 (source=answer); resume replay returned status=complete turns=1 decisions=4 with turn 0 carrying 4 structured questions. Service test 'resume replays ... with NO new generation' asserts the mock call count is unchanged.

items.title: Materiality gate + max-rounds cap + stop-and-generate drive termination
steps: 1. After answering a batch whose hasOpenMaterialDecisions is false, POST next-batch and confirm the interview terminates (status=complete, isComplete=true) with no new generation.
2. Confirm the round count never exceeds INTERVIEW_ENGINE.MAX_ROUNDS even when decisions remain open.
3. Submit answers with stopAndGenerate=true and confirm immediate completion.
items.expected: The gate generates another batch only when an open decision is material; the hard cap and the global stop both force termination at shared understanding.
items.evidence: Live run terminated at round 1 (gate reason 'no_material_decisions'). MaterialityGateService tests cover first_batch, open_material_decisions, no_material_decisions, max_rounds_reached, stopped_by_user (6 pass). Service tests cover terminate-via-gate and stop-and-generate.

items.title: Engine endpoints return the {success,data,error} envelope and reject bad input at the boundary
items.precondition: App running; owner scope set via x-dev-user-id header (never the body).
steps: 1. GET /sessions/:id/interview → 200 + {success:true,data,error:null}.
2. POST .../answers with answers:'not-an-array' → 400 + error.code VALIDATION_ERROR.
3. GET interview for a missing id → 404 SESSION_NOT_FOUND; for another owner's session → 404 (no leak, §11.7); answer with no open batch → 409 NO_OPEN_BATCH_CONFLICT.
4. Run a full multi-round interview over HTTP to termination.
items.expected: Every endpoint returns the canonical envelope; invalid bodies/params are rejected at the boundary; cross-owner reads 404; a full HTTP run drives to status=complete.
items.evidence: InterviewEngineController.test.ts (7 supertest tests) pass: envelope on GET, 400 VALIDATION_ERROR, 404 SESSION_NOT_FOUND (missing + cross-owner), 409 NO_OPEN_BATCH_CONFLICT, and a full multi-round HTTP run ending status=complete/isComplete=true.

items.title: Frontend question-batch step renders the batch, collects answers (+ other + stop), via the api/ client
items.precondition: Frontend built; backend reachable through the Vite /api proxy; a session created in step 1 of the wizard.
steps: 1. In InterviewWizard, create a session (step 1), then on step 2 click 'Generate questions'.
2. The QuestionBatch step renders each question with options; pick an option or the free-text 'other'.
3. Click 'Submit answers' to advance, or 'Stop and generate now' to end; confirm the wizard reaches the completion step.
4. Confirm no raw fetch/axios outside api/index.ts (§14.2) and no `any` (§17.2).
items.expected: Answering a batch advances to the next batch and finally to termination; all data flows through the api/ client and React Query hooks; errors surface via the shared toast.
items.evidence: Browser acceptance pass 2026-06-24 (dev server localhost:59436, real model). Resumed a scoped session into the interview step; clicked 'Generate questions' -> POST /sessions/880/interview/next-batch 200 rendered a 4-question batch (survey_channel, timing_config, audience_segmentation, branching_logic), each with radio options + an 'Other (describe)' free-text. Answered all 4 using the free-text 'other' on q2 ('Hybrid: admin sets a base delay...'); 'Submit answers' -> POST .../answers 200 persisted turn0 with 4 answers + 4 decision_record rows (q2 decision correctly stored otherText, not optionId). Clicked 'Generate questions' again -> materiality gate generated a distinct round-2 batch (storage, over-surveying, who-configures, opt-outs). Answered round 2 and clicked 'Stop and generate now' -> POST .../answers (stop) 200 terminated the interview (isComplete=true) and the wizard auto-advanced to the Ticket step. Verified visually via preview screenshot (4-question batch with radios + Other) and via the live POST .../answers response body (turn0 answers + 4 decisions). All data flowed through the api/ client; no console errors.
items.notes: Full two-round interview driven in a real browser to termination via stop-and-generate; write-through persistence (turns + decisions incl. free-text other) confirmed against the live state payload.

items.title: Rev 4: Dashboard reads neutral — all action buttons white/neutral, only the active filter pill is accent
items.precondition: Frontend built; backend reachable via the Vite /api proxy; dashboard has sessions.
steps: 1. Load the dashboard.
2. Read computed background-color of New session, Resume/Finish, View ticket, Re-run buttons.
3. Read the active 'All' filter pill background.
4. Read the IN PROGRESS and COMPLETE status badge backgrounds.
items.expected: Every dashboard action button bg = surface rgb(18,20,28) with white ink, none = accent rgb(255,117,31). Active 'All' pill = accent. IN PROGRESS badge = translucent white rgba(255,255,255,0.08). COMPLETE badge stays green.
items.evidence: preview_inspect computed styles (2026-06-25, localhost web dev server): all action buttons bg rgb(18,20,28), color rgb(244,245,248), isAccentBg=false. Filter pills: only active 'All' = rgb(255,117,31); others neutral. Badges: in_progress bg rgba(255,255,255,0.08) color rgb(244,245,248); complete color rgb(110,231,176) green. Built CSS rule: session-status-in_progress{color:var(--ink);background:#ffffff14;border-color:#ffffff2e} — no accent.

items.title: Rev 4: Session row shows the title as primary label; raw request is a muted subtitle (request fallback when title null)
steps: 1. Inspect .session-title (primary) and .session-snippet (subtitle) in each row.
2. Confirm title is white display font weight 600; subtitle is muted.
3. With title null, confirm the request shows as the primary label and no duplicate subtitle is rendered.
items.expected: Title is the primary label (ink, Fraunces, 600); request is a muted subtitle when a title exists; when title is null the request becomes the primary label with no echoed subtitle.
items.evidence: preview_eval: .session-title color rgb(244,245,248), font Fraunces, weight 600. Live sessions have title=null (backend title feature newly added) so they correctly fall back to the request as primary with subtitle hidden (verified live). .session-snippet CSS rule = color var(--muted), 0.9rem (the subtitle styling for the title-present branch). primaryLabel/subtitle logic in SessionList.tsx.
items.notes: No live session currently has a non-null title, so the title-present subtitle branch was verified via CSS rule + code, not a populated row.

items.title: Rev 4: Logo in the app header + favicon
steps: 1. Confirm <link rel=icon href=/logo.png> in index.html.
2. Inspect .wizard-brand .wizard-logo on dashboard, wizard, and ticket headers.
items.expected: Favicon href is /logo.png; the logo renders 32x32 beside the PM Ticket Tool wordmark in the header.
items.evidence: preview_eval: faviconHref=/logo.png; .wizard-brand .wizard-logo src=/logo.png, 32x32, visible on dashboard and wizard headers. index.html has the icon link.

items.title: Rev 4: Triage loader is an animated gauge that resolves to a GREEN check before routing onward
steps: 1. Submit a request and observe the 'Sizing up your request' panel.
2. Confirm the loader shows animated bars (accent gradient, scaleY animation) and a rotating phase line.
3. On completion confirm a green check shows briefly (SETTLE_HOLD_MS ~950ms) before the result panel reveals.
items.expected: Animated gauge while classifying; a green check (COMPLETE green) on completion with a short hold; then the result reveals (no snap).
items.evidence: preview_eval probe of the loader DOM: .triage-bar backgroundImage = linear-gradient(rgb(255,143,71),rgb(255,117,31)) (accent), 5px wide, framer scaleY keyframes; .triage-check color rgb(110,231,176) (green), green-tinted bg + border, isGreenish=true. TriageBranch holds on the check for SETTLE_HOLD_MS=950ms before revealing the result (the result heading appears after a delay). The transient is sub-second so it was verified by deterministic DOM/CSS probe + the settle-gate code rather than a frame capture.
items.notes: Triage resolves faster than discrete polling could catch the check frame; verified structurally.

items.title: Rev 4: No-spinner loading states now use an animated indicator
items.precondition: A state that loads interview/session data.
steps: 1. Trigger the interview-state load (QuestionBatch) and the dashboard sessions load.
2. Confirm a LoadingLine (spinning ring + label) renders instead of bare text.
items.expected: Loading states render an animated spinning ring + label ('Loading feature scope…', 'Loading sessions…'), not static text.
items.evidence: LoadingLine component (components/ui/LoadingLine.tsx) renders a framer-motion rotating ring + label; wired into QuestionBatch (Loading feature scope…) and Dashboard (Loading sessions…). CSS .loading-line-spinner present in built bundle. Bare strings 'Loading interview…' removed (0 occurrences in built JS).

items.title: Rev 4: Batch loader includes playful Dave phrases mixed with functional ones
steps: 1. Click generate and watch the BatchThinking loader label rotate.
2. Confirm Dave-themed phrases appear among the functional ones.
items.expected: The loader rotates through a mix of functional lines and Dave/engineer asides.
items.evidence: Live capture (preview_eval on a fresh dev server): batch loader label showed 'Dave is grepping the codebase…' during generation. Built JS bundle contains all Dave phrases (Dave put down coffee, grepping, renamed a variable, arguing with the linter, found a TODO from 2019, rebasing, worked on his machine).

items.title: Rev 4: Carousel option cards + build-speed meter fit within the slide; nothing is clipped (incl. hover/select)
items.precondition: An open batch rendered in the carousel.
steps: 1. At a wide viewport (2-column grid) measure each option card and its [role=meter] right edge against the carousel stage and viewport.
2. Select the right-column card (scale 1.03) and re-measure.
3. Confirm every meter renders all 5 segments and stays within the stage.
items.expected: No card or meter overflows the stage/viewport at any breakpoint; all 5-segment meters render fully, including when a card is hovered/selected.
items.evidence: preview_eval bounding-box measurements at 1100px and 1024px (2-column): anyCardOverflowsStage=false, anyCardOverflowsViewport=false, all meters segs=5 withinStage=true. Right-column selected card (aria-pressed=true, scale 1.03) right=954 < stage 968 < viewport — not clipped; its meter right=933 fully rendered. At 369px single-column, same (no clipping). Fix: .carousel-stage overflow:visible + 8px/14px padding (parent .step-panel clips overshoot); cards given min-w-0.

items.title: Rev 4: Custom-answer selection is mutually exclusive (typing custom selects it + de-selects the option; clearing reverts to none)
items.precondition: An open batch with options + an 'Something else' free-text card.
steps: 1. Select an option; confirm it gets the full selected treatment and others recede.
2. Type into the custom free-text; confirm the custom card gets the full selected treatment (accent border + check + shadow) AND the previously-selected option de-highlights.
3. Clear the custom input; confirm selection reverts to none.
items.expected: Selection is exclusive: option vs custom never both selected; typing custom takes the selection with full visual treatment and clears the option; clearing custom reverts to no selection.
items.evidence: preview_eval sequence on a fresh dev server (clean CSS, 2026-06-25): (A) select option[1] -> pressed=true, accentBorder=true, opacity 1; others + custom opacity 0.45. (B) type into custom -> custom card accentBorder=true, accentShadow=true, opacity 1; ALL options pressed=false, opacity 0.45 (prior option de-highlighted). (C) clear custom -> custom accentBorder=false opacity 1, all options pressed=false opacity ~1 (no selection). Also verified the recommended card when selected paints full accent (rgb(255,117,31)), not the /55 recommended border. Logic in QuestionBatch.setOption/setOther; visual treatment in OptionDeck.OtherCard.
items.notes: An initial measurement on a stale pre-edit dev server showed correct className/aria-pressed but stale CSS paint; restarting the dev server (fresh Tailwind generation) confirmed the paint, matching the verified production bundle.

items.title: Rev 4: 'Interview' renamed to 'Feature Scope' in all user-facing copy
steps: 1. Read the stepper labels, the request-entry submit button + hint, the triage result copy + buttons, and the batch eyebrow.
2. Confirm no user-facing 'interview' string remains; backend/internal identifiers may keep 'interview'.
items.expected: Stepper shows 'Feature Scope'; buttons read 'Start feature scoping'; batch eyebrow reads 'Feature Scope'; triage/hint copy say 'feature scope'. No visible 'interview'.
items.evidence: preview_eval live: step rail = [Request, Feature Scope, Ticket]; request submit = 'Start feature scoping'; request hint = '...through a short feature scope.'; triage hint = '...walk through a short feature scope...'; triage primary button = 'Start feature scoping'; batch eyebrow = 'Feature Scope'; GenerateBatchPanel button = 'Start feature scoping'. Built JS: 'Feature Scope' x2, 'Start feature scoping' present; old 'Start interview'/'Start the interview'/'Loading interview' = 0 occurrences.

items.title: Rev 4: No em-dashes (or en-dashes) remain in shipped user-facing strings
steps: 1. Scan all .ts/.tsx string literals and JSX text for em/en dashes (excluding code comments).
2. Fix any found in rendered copy.
items.expected: Zero em/en dashes in any shipped string or JSX text; comments may retain them (stripped from the bundle).
items.evidence: grep of all string literals + JSX text nodes across frontend/src returned zero em/en dashes after fixes. Three genuine string-literal dashes were fixed: SpeedMeter aria-label, QuestionBatch 'Nice, those are saved...', TicketView EFFORT_NOTE. All remaining dashes are in JSDoc/inline/CSS comments (allowed, and stripped from the production bundle).
